Ulasan: Petty and rather unprofessional  just go. visit once or twice and you'll realize a few things about the members:  1) petty - they aren't there to promote a good service, they are their for #1 see how many referrals they can snatch from the group. If they think you or someone you know provides a service someone else in the group does, you aren't welcome  2) desperate - come on, you see hungry lawyers, masseuses, chiropractors, therapists, life coaches, dog walkers.... these are a dime a dozen trades. some are obviously BS services. Some i'm sure are successful (granted, the ones who attended for DECADES) but you can see desperation in most members' faces  3) unprofessional - forget the fact that you have to reintroduce yourself every time you go (like a child standing up to speak to the class).  The team leaders just aren't properly prepared to deal with conflicts; rather than train or coach their members to be more professional, they take the easy route and chase away whoever is newest. Let's not forget that their speeches contradict their actions about striving to provide quality service, not quantity referrals.
